title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: Today is moving day. We're moving from Seoul to Busan, so I'm going to teach you some English expressions used while moving, starting from packing and preparing all the way to enjoying Cha Jung-myun in our new place. We spent the week sorting through our things and packing all our belongings into boxes. We used bubble wrap to protect anything fragile in labeled each box so we'd know exactly where everything should go later. We emptied out our storage unit and since we're trying to live a more minimalist lifestyle, we got rid of as much as we could by selling things on Dangan. We hired a moving company, so on moving day, the mover helped us carry our boxes and clear out the apartment. We did one last walk through and set our final goodbye. He loaded everything into the back of the truck and strapped it down to keep it secure during the ride. We're in the moving truck now on our way to Busan. It's about a five-hour drive.

When moving, you always need a box cutter. These things come in handy. I'm tipping the box over. Let's use these muscles. Okay, I got this. I feel like it's going to explode if like the tent at the Han. Done. Let's test it out. Wow. It looks a little bit sad right now. We don't have a bed frame yet and it's quite thin because it was vacuum sealed, so it's still expanding. But it's not bad. It's pretty comfortable. I'm unpacking boxes and getting organized. For such a small apartment, there's surprisingly a lot of storage, so I'm figuring out where to put everything.

I'm obsessed. I'll try to do even more cooking lives or cooking videos for you guys. It's been a long and exhausting day. So I'm going to finish eating my dinner and watch a show and call it a night. If you have any other moving vocabulary or expressions that you're curious about, leave them in the comments below and I'll make sure to answer your questions. I'm being resourceful.

title: will / shall
examples: It's quite a long trip from Seoul to Busan, so we're taking a quick break and grabbing a bite to eat at the rest stop. title: have to / must
examples: We've got our work cut out for us tonight. This apartment is a bit small but it's cozy. It's time to start unpacking. I hate this part. Our last apartment was fully furnished, so we didn't have any of our own furniture, but this place was completely empty. It was unfurnished, so we had to buy a few necessities like this mattress. We need somewhere to sleep tonight. Let's do some unboxing.
